evals.prompt: Our WGS lab is still on CPU and each sample takes most of a day from FASTQ to a sorted, deduped, recalibrated BAM. We already have aligned BAMs from an older run and need BQSR tables applied before variant calling on Parabricks 4.7. Which pbrun steps should we use?
evals.expected_output: Recommends applybqsr for applying existing recalibration tables, notes when a separate bqsr step is needed to generate tables, confirms Parabricks version/container, identifies BAM and recalibration inputs, and notes fq2bam for new FASTQ alignments rather than applybqsr alone.

assertions.description: Recommends pbrun applybqsr for applying BQSR to existing aligned BAMs
assertions.check: Response contains 'applybqsr'

assertions.description: Identifies BAM/CRAM paths and BQSR recalibration table as required inputs
assertions.check: judge: Identifies BAM/CRAM paths and BQSR recalibration table as required inputs

assertions.description: Notes that new FASTQ-to-BAM alignment should use fq2bam rather than applybqsr alone
assertions.check: judge: Notes that new FASTQ-to-BAM alignment should use fq2bam rather than applybqsr alone

evals.prompt: hey — we've got bisulfite WGBS paired FASTQs (human, GRCh38) and want methylation-aware alignments on GPU with Parabricks. what command should we start with?
evals.expected_output: Recommends fq2bam_meth, lists methylation-specific reference preparation expectations, identifies FASTQ and reference inputs, and warns against standard fq2bam for bisulfite data.

assertions.description: Recommends pbrun fq2bam_meth for bisulfite/methylation FASTQ alignment
assertions.check: Response contains 'fq2bam_meth'

assertions.description: Mentions reference or bisulfite/methylation preparation requirements
assertions.check: judge: Mentions reference or bisulfite/methylation preparation requirements

assertions.description: Identifies or asks for FASTQ paths and reference before finalizing a command
assertions.check: judge: Identifies or asks for FASTQ paths and reference before finalizing a command

assertions.description: Does not recommend standard pbrun fq2bam for bisulfite data without a warning
assertions.check: judge: Does not recommend standard pbrun fq2bam for bisulfite data without a warning

evals.prompt: We have Oxford Nanopore WGS FASTQ for one sample and need GPU alignment to BAM before germline calling. Parabricks is new to us — which preprocessing/alignment tool fits ONT, and what do we run after alignment for variants?
evals.expected_output: Recommends minimap2 for ONT FASTQ alignment with long-read context, notes version-specific flags, and recommends ont_germline or another germline caller as a follow-on step rather than short-read fq2bam.

assertions.description: Recommends pbrun minimap2 for ONT FASTQ alignment
assertions.check: Response contains 'minimap2'

assertions.description: Mentions long-read or ONT context rather than short-read fq2bam alone
assertions.check: judge: Mentions long-read or ONT context rather than short-read fq2bam alone

assertions.description: Recommends a germline variant-calling follow-on (e.g. ont_germline)
assertions.check: judge: Recommends a germline variant-calling follow-on (e.g. ont_germline)

assertions.description: Asks to confirm Parabricks version or container tag
assertions.check: judge: Asks to confirm Parabricks version or container tag

evals.prompt: We have ordinary 30x human WGS paired FASTQs, but someone wrote 'RNA' in the project folder name. I want the fastest Parabricks FASTQ-to-BAM path and downstream germline variants. Should I use rna_fq2bam because of the folder name?
evals.expected_output: Rejects routing based on folder name alone, recommends fq2bam for DNA WGS FASTQ, explains rna_fq2bam is for RNA-seq only, and recommends a germline caller for downstream variants.

assertions.description: Recommends pbrun fq2bam for ordinary DNA WGS FASTQ-to-BAM
assertions.check: Response contains 'fq2bam'

assertions.description: Does not route to rna_fq2bam based only on a folder or project name containing RNA
assertions.check: judge: Does not route to rna_fq2bam based only on a folder or project name containing RNA

assertions.description: Identifies or asks for FASTQ, reference, read group, or known-sites inputs
assertions.check: judge: Identifies or asks for FASTQ, reference, read group, or known-sites inputs

assertions.description: Recommends a germline variant caller for downstream variants
assertions.check: judge: Recommends a germline variant caller for downstream variants

evals.prompt: Sequencing finished and fq2bam just wrote BAMs. Before we spend GPU hours on variant calling, how do we check if coverage and quality are good enough for a go/no-go on Parabricks 4.7?
evals.expected_output: Recommends bammetrics for WGS coverage/QC thresholds or collectmultiplemetrics for broader Picard-style metrics, frames the response as a QC go/no-go decision, and identifies BAM and reference inputs.

assertions.description: Recommends bammetrics or collectmultiplemetrics (or both with tradeoffs)
assertions.check: judge: Recommends bammetrics or collectmultiplemetrics (or both with tradeoffs)

assertions.description: Frames response as a QC or go/no-go decision before downstream analysis
assertions.check: judge: Frames response as a QC or go/no-go decision before downstream analysis

assertions.description: Identifies or asks for BAM/CRAM and reference FASTA inputs
assertions.check: judge: Identifies or asks for BAM/CRAM and reference FASTA inputs

evals.prompt: QC time — whole genome cram, need insert size + gc bias + artifact metrics like CollectMultipleMetrics used to give us. one docker run if possible?
evals.expected_output: Recommends collectmultiplemetrics, notes available metric families, identifies CRAM and reference inputs, and does not recommend bammetrics alone when multiple metric families are requested.

assertions.description: Recommends pbrun collectmultiplemetrics
assertions.check: judge: Recommends pbrun collectmultiplemetrics

assertions.description: Mentions multiple metric types (e.g. insert size, GC bias, or artifacts)
assertions.check: judge: Mentions multiple metric types (e.g. insert size, GC bias, or artifacts)

assertions.description: Identifies or asks for alignment file and reference paths
assertions.check: judge: Identifies or asks for alignment file and reference paths

assertions.description: Does not recommend bammetrics alone when user asked for multiple metric families
assertions.check: judge: Does not recommend bammetrics alone when user asked for multiple metric families

evals.prompt: For a 30x WGS BAM, we only care whether mean coverage is above 25x and pct bases above Q20 — what's the lightest Parabricks QC command?
evals.expected_output: Recommends bammetrics for coverage/threshold-style metrics, connects the recommendation to coverage or quality thresholds, and identifies BAM and reference inputs.

assertions.description: Recommends pbrun bammetrics
assertions.check: judge: Recommends pbrun bammetrics

assertions.description: Connects recommendation to coverage or quality thresholds
assertions.check: judge: Connects recommendation to coverage or quality thresholds

assertions.description: Identifies or asks for BAM/CRAM path and reference
assertions.check: judge: Identifies or asks for BAM/CRAM path and reference

assertions.description: Does not route variant calling tools for a QC-only question
assertions.check: judge: Does not route variant calling tools for a QC-only question

evals.prompt: The BAM has low coverage in a few targets. Can bammetrics fix the variants or should I run haplotypecaller from the QC category?
evals.expected_output: Explains QC tools only report metrics and do not fix variants, does not treat haplotypecaller as a QC tool, and recommends haplotypecaller or another variant caller when recall is the goal.

assertions.description: Explains that bammetrics or collectmultiplemetrics report QC metrics and do not fix variants
assertions.check: judge: Explains that bammetrics and/or collectmultiplemetrics are QC or metrics tools that report statistics and do not fix variants, modify BAMs, or improve coverage

assertions.description: Does not route haplotypecaller as a quality-control tool
assertions.check: judge: Does not route haplotypecaller as a quality-control tool

assertions.description: Recommends a variant caller (e.g. haplotypecaller) when variant recall is the goal
assertions.check: Response contains 'haplotypecaller'

assertions.description: Rejects the idea of running haplotypecaller from a QC category to fix coverage or variants
assertions.check: judge: Rejects the idea of running haplotypecaller from a QC category to fix coverage or variants

evals.prompt: New RNA-seq project: paired tumor RNA FASTQs, GRCh38, we need splice-aware alignment to BAM and later fusion detection. We're on Parabricks in Docker with an A100. Walk me through which RNA tools to use first vs second.
evals.expected_output: Sequences rna_fq2bam first for splice-aware alignment, then starfusion for fusion detection, and asks for STAR genome library and STAR-Fusion resource paths.

assertions.description: Recommends rna_fq2bam before fusion calling
assertions.check: judge: Recommends rna_fq2bam before fusion calling

assertions.description: Mentions starfusion for fusion detection
assertions.check: judge: Mentions starfusion for fusion detection

assertions.description: Asks for STAR genome library or STAR-Fusion genome resource inputs
assertions.check: judge: Asks for STAR genome library or STAR-Fusion genome resource inputs

evals.prompt: I only have Chimeric.out.junction and Chimeric.out.sam from STAR on disk — can Parabricks call fusions from that, and which command?
evals.expected_output: Recommends starfusion directly, references chimeric junction inputs, asks for STAR-Fusion genome library path, and does not require rna_fq2bam when junction files already exist.

assertions.description: Recommends pbrun starfusion
assertions.check: judge: Recommends pbrun starfusion

assertions.description: References chimeric junction or STAR-Fusion style inputs
assertions.check: judge: References chimeric junction or STAR-Fusion style inputs

assertions.description: Asks for STAR-Fusion genome resource library path or equivalent
assertions.check: judge: Asks for STAR-Fusion genome resource library path or equivalent

assertions.description: Does not require rna_fq2bam when junction files are already provided
assertions.check: judge: Does not require rna_fq2bam when junction files are already provided

evals.prompt: need gpu rna align — 2x150 PE human blood, have ref fasta + star index tarball. not sure if we should markdup. pb 4.7 container on slurm.
evals.expected_output: Recommends rna_fq2bam with STAR library handling, identifies FASTQ and genome-lib-dir inputs, discusses duplicate marking only if appropriate for RNA, and notes runtime/Slurm GPU setup if relevant.

assertions.description: Recommends pbrun rna_fq2bam
assertions.check: judge: Recommends pbrun rna_fq2bam

assertions.description: Mentions STAR genome library or --genome-lib-dir
assertions.check: judge: Mentions STAR genome library or --genome-lib-dir

assertions.description: Identifies or asks for FASTQ and output paths rather than inventing sample names
assertions.check: judge: Identifies or asks for FASTQ and output paths rather than inventing sample names

assertions.description: Does not confuse RNA alignment with DNA pbrun fq2bam
assertions.check: judge: Does not confuse RNA alignment with DNA pbrun fq2bam

evals.prompt: We have raw RNA tumor FASTQs and want fusion calls. Can you give me one pbrun starfusion command that takes the FASTQs directly?
evals.expected_output: States starfusion does not take raw FASTQs directly, routes first to rna_fq2bam to produce chimeric junction output, then starfusion with STAR-Fusion genome library inputs.

assertions.description: States that pbrun starfusion does not take raw FASTQs directly in the documented workflow
assertions.check: judge: States that pbrun starfusion does not take raw FASTQs directly in the documented workflow

assertions.description: Routes raw RNA FASTQs first to rna_fq2bam
assertions.check: judge: Routes raw RNA FASTQs first to rna_fq2bam

assertions.description: Mentions chimeric junction input as required or expected for starfusion
assertions.check: judge: Mentions chimeric junction input as required or expected for starfusion

assertions.description: Asks for STAR genome library and STAR-Fusion genome resource paths
assertions.check: judge: Asks for STAR genome library and STAR-Fusion genome resource paths

evals.prompt: Clinical lab: sorted, deduped, BQSR'd WGS BAM (GRCh38) from NovaSeq — we need germline small variants with high sensitivity/specificity on Parabricks. HaplotypeCaller gVCF vs germline pipeline vs DeepVariant — what should we pick?
evals.expected_output: Compares germline, haplotypecaller, deepvariant, and deepvariant_germline options with tradeoffs, recommends at least one caller for prepared BAM input, and identifies BAM and reference/known-sites context.

assertions.description: Discusses more than one germline calling option (e.g. germline, haplotypecaller, or deepvariant)
assertions.check: judge: Discusses more than one germline calling option (e.g. germline, haplotypecaller, or deepvariant)

assertions.description: Recommends at least one specific germline caller for prepared BAM input
assertions.check: judge: Recommends at least one specific germline caller for prepared BAM input

assertions.description: Identifies or asks for BAM input and reference/known-sites context
assertions.check: judge: Identifies or asks for BAM input and reference/known-sites context

evals.prompt: Tumor/normal FFPE exome — matched normal BAMs are ready. Need somatic SNV/indel calling on GPU with artifact filtering suitable for oncology reports. Which Parabricks somatic path?
evals.expected_output: Recommends mutectcaller, somatic, or deepsomatic with tumor-normal context, identifies sample IDs and BAM paths, and provides version-aware guidance for the chosen caller.

assertions.description: Recommends a somatic caller (mutectcaller, somatic, or deepsomatic)
assertions.check: judge: Recommends a somatic caller (mutectcaller, somatic, or deepsomatic)

assertions.description: Mentions tumor-normal or matched normal context
assertions.check: judge: Mentions tumor-normal or matched normal context

assertions.description: Provides version-aware command guidance for the recommended somatic caller
assertions.check: judge: Provides version-aware command guidance for the recommended somatic caller

assertions.description: Identifies or asks for sample metadata or BAM paths
assertions.check: judge: Identifies or asks for sample metadata or BAM paths

evals.prompt: Population genomics cohort on GRCh38 — linear reference bias in complex regions is a concern. We have graph resources and want pangenome-aware germline calling. Where do prepon/postpon fit vs pangenome_germline?
evals.expected_output: Explains pangenome-aware flow (prepon, pangenome_germline or pangenome_aware_deepvariant, postpon as applicable), identifies graph/reference resource inputs, and does not recommend only linear haplotypecaller without noting pangenome options.

assertions.description: Mentions pangenome-aware tools such as prepon, pangenome_germline, or pangenome_aware_deepvariant
assertions.check: judge: Mentions pangenome-aware tools such as prepon, pangenome_germline, or pangenome_aware_deepvariant

assertions.description: References graph or pangenome resources as required inputs
assertions.check: judge: References graph or pangenome resources as required inputs

assertions.description: Explains how prepon and/or postpon relate to pangenome_germline in the workflow
assertions.check: judge: Explains how prepon and/or postpon relate to pangenome_germline in the workflow

assertions.description: Does not recommend only linear-reference haplotypecaller without noting pangenome options
assertions.check: judge: Does not recommend only linear-reference haplotypecaller without noting pangenome options

evals.prompt: I only have a joint cohort VCF and want rsID annotation, but I was told variant calling skills know everything. Should I run haplotypecaller again?
evals.expected_output: Does not rerun read-level variant callers for annotation-only VCF work, recommends dbsnp for annotation, asks for input VCF and dbSNP resource build compatibility.

assertions.description: Does not recommend haplotypecaller or another read-level caller for annotation-only VCF work
assertions.check: judge: Does not recommend haplotypecaller or another read-level caller for annotation-only VCF work

assertions.description: Recommends pbrun dbsnp for dbSNP annotation
assertions.check: judge: Recommends pbrun dbsnp for dbSNP annotation

assertions.description: Asks for input VCF and dbSNP resource or reference build compatibility
assertions.check: judge: Asks for input VCF and dbSNP resource or reference build compatibility

assertions.description: Explains that the task is post-calling annotation rather than variant calling
assertions.check: judge: Explains that the task is post-calling annotation rather than variant calling

evals.prompt: We have per-sample gVCFs from HaplotypeCaller on 48 unrelated exomes (GRCh38) and need joint genotyping on Parabricks before VCF filtering. What's the right command?
evals.expected_output: Recommends genotypegvcf, mentions cohort or multiple GVCF inputs, identifies reference build and GVCF paths, and does not recommend read-level variant callers for joint genotyping only.

assertions.description: Recommends pbrun genotypegvcf
assertions.check: judge: Recommends pbrun genotypegvcf

assertions.description: Mentions cohort or multiple GVCF inputs
assertions.check: judge: Mentions cohort or multiple GVCF inputs

assertions.description: Identifies or asks for reference build or GVCF paths
assertions.check: judge: Identifies or asks for reference build or GVCF paths

assertions.description: Does not recommend variant callers (germline/haplotypecaller) for joint genotyping only
assertions.check: judge: Does not recommend variant callers (germline/haplotypecaller) for joint genotyping only

evals.prompt: Joint genotyping failed because gvcf index missing — do we index first with Parabricks and which tool?
evals.expected_output: Recommends indexgvcf before genotypegvcf, explains the index-then-genotype sequence, and identifies GVCF path or format details.

assertions.description: Recommends pbrun indexgvcf
assertions.check: judge: Recommends pbrun indexgvcf

assertions.description: Mentions genotypegvcf as a follow-on step after indexing
assertions.check: judge: Mentions genotypegvcf as a follow-on step after indexing

assertions.description: Identifies or asks for GVCF path or format details
assertions.check: judge: Identifies or asks for GVCF path or format details

evals.prompt: After joint calling we have a project VCF — need dbSNP IDs annotated for rsIDs before delivery. Parabricks 4.7, hg38 dbsnp vcf on disk.
evals.expected_output: Recommends dbsnp, mentions dbSNP resource compatibility with reference build, asks for input VCF path, and does not recommend genotypegvcf for annotation-only work.

assertions.description: Recommends pbrun dbsnp
assertions.check: judge: Recommends pbrun dbsnp

assertions.description: Mentions dbSNP resource or annotation compatibility with reference build
assertions.check: judge: Mentions dbSNP resource or annotation compatibility with reference build

assertions.description: Identifies or asks for input VCF path or build confirmation
assertions.check: judge: Identifies or asks for input VCF path or build confirmation

assertions.description: Does not recommend genotypegvcf for annotation-only task
assertions.check: judge: Does not recommend genotypegvcf for annotation-only task

evals.prompt: We have BAMs from 20 samples and want to jointly genotype them. Can genotypegvcf call variants directly from BAM, or do we skip per-sample gVCFs?
evals.expected_output: States genotypegvcf works on gVCF inputs not BAMs, recommends per-sample variant calling first, then indexgvcf/genotypegvcf for joint genotyping.

assertions.description: States that genotypegvcf expects gVCF inputs rather than BAM inputs
assertions.check: judge: States that genotypegvcf expects gVCF inputs rather than BAM inputs

assertions.description: Recommends per-sample variant calling before joint genotyping

assertions.check: judge: Mentions per-sample gVCFs as required intermediate inputs for genotypegvcf
